title: hieracium creperum
intro: Hieracium creperum, commonly known as Creeping Hawkweed, is a low-growing, herbaceous perennial plant belonging to the dandelion family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Hieracium creperum include its potential to aid in the treatment of skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its ability to act as an antiseptic and antibacterial agent. Herbal preparations of Hieracium creperum are typically made by infusing the leaves and roots in hot water to create a tea, which can be used to treat various ailments, or by drying the plant to make a tincture or capsules for oral consumption.
